title: LinearMarkingMenu Object
description: Represents the linear marking menu which is the vertical menu that's displayed when the user right-clicks within Fusion. This supports customizing the contents of the context menu.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Completely clears the contents of the context menu. If left in this state, the context menu will not be displayed.

properties.name: controls
properties.description: Return the collection of top-level controls in the context menu. It's possible to have drop-down controls (fly-outs) that provide access to additional controls. You can remove and add controls to customize the contents of the context menu.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

classType.title: LinearMarkingMenu.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

clear.title: LinearMarkingMenu.clear Method
clear.description: Completely clears the contents of the context menu. If left in this state, the context menu will not be displayed.
clear.return_description: Returns true if the clear was successful.

related_samples.name: Marking Menu API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates how to customize marking menu and context menu. This sample is an add-in. To use it, create a new add-in using the "Scrips and Add-Ins" command. Use any name you would like for the add-in. In the folder where the add-in was created edit the add-in name.py file and replace it's entire contents with the sample code below. You can also delete all the other files that were created for the add-in except for add-in name.manifiest. Start the add-in from the "Scripts and Add-Ins" dialog. Now, with the add-in running, whenever you right-click in the Fusion window, you'll get an entirely customized context menu. The default marking menu has been modified by the add-in by removing the existing commands and adding some custom commands.

controls.title: LinearMarkingMenu.controls Property
controls.description: Return the collection of top-level controls in the context menu. It's possible to have drop-down controls (fly-outs) that provide access to additional controls. You can remove and add controls to customize the contents of the context menu.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = linearMarkingMenu_var.controls
